The double circuit low of 0.915 sits below the single circuit low of 1.064, which is not physically expected. It illustrates ESMAP's point that cross-study comparison is unreliable without scope normalisation.

scope: operation and maintenance as reported in World Bank project documents
notes: Below the wider literature, which reports 5 to 10 percent (Larsen 2016 as cited by ESMAP). A submission using 5 to 10 percent is not wrong, but should say which basis it follows.

scope: discount rates applied in World Bank project appraisal
notes: Clusters at 6 to 12 percent, consistent with concessional financing. A privately financed IPT would be expected above this band, so do not flag a higher figure without checking the financing structure.

scope: incidence of implementation delay, principal causes procurement and land
locator: Introduction and Figure 3.11, Causes of Implementation Delays
notes: Nearly 70 percent of the sample was delayed, yet about half still closed below the appraisal estimate. Use for the optimism-bias test on schedule, and note that the same does not hold for cost.

scope: OHL supply and erection only. Excludes substation, contingency, consultancy, ESIA/RAP, land and compensation.
locator: Table 8, Project costs estimates for the Transmission line options
notes: Flat rate applied identically across the 123, 141 and 152 km options, so it carries no terrain differentiation. Benchmarks pre-feasibility practice, not feasibility practice. Project data: check TOR Section 14 before any external use.
Identical across all three route options, so it is a placeholder rather than a costed design. Do not use as a benchmark. Held for traceability only.
Rule of thumb. Advisory only.

scope: land requirement, implies an effective corridor of about 35 m against a stated 30 m right of way
locator: Table 8, derived across the three route options
notes: Consistent across all three options, which is what makes it usable. Cross-check against the stated 30 m RoW.

scope: resettlement and compensation, cash or land
notes: Round-number rule of thumb at 2020 prices. Must be replaced by a valuation survey at feasibility.
A convention, not a risk-derived contingency. AACE requires contingency to follow from risk analysis. Flag a flat 10 percent at feasibility.

scope: total project capex of USD 432M over 288.47 km. Whole-project scope, NOT line-only. Not comparable with line_capex_per_km entries.
locator: Pre-feasibility cost estimate, stated at plus or minus 35 percent
notes: Scope differs from the literature line-only entries, so the metric name differs deliberately. The stated plus or minus 35 percent is symmetric and matches no AACE class shape, which suggests it was asserted rather than risk-derived. See reference_tables.aace.
Equivalent to 0.0032 USD/kWh. ESMAP proposes LCOT as a reference for wheeling where a regulated tariff is missing or unreliable. Compute LCOT and compare once the LCOT method is implemented.
